documents.text: Ares Advance Practice SOAP Note 5660 W Flamingo Rd Ste B, Las Vegas, NV 89103-2312 CORA ANTONIO MRN: Birthday : 1933-03-03 Phone : 8315949837 Visited on: 2026 Apr 15 11:16 (Age at visit: 93 years) Electronically signed by: MILAGROS MICAELA MENDOZA, APRN-CNP on 2026-04-16 12:30 PM CC Establish Care: Establishment of care Initiation of Visit: This visit was initiated by patient's son. The chief complaint is needing an in-home healthcare provider. The first contact date with patient is 04/14/2026. MEDICAL NECESSITY: This visit with a FTF encounter with the patient in the home is not a visit of convenience. Patient is seen in the home because there is a medical necessity for this visit to be conducted in the patient's environment. The patient suffers from recent with pain exacerbated by activity which is contraindicated for the patient to be seen in an office setting today. It is medically necessary and safer for the patient to be seen in the patient's own environment today. Subjective This is a 93 year old Female seen today for initial visit and establishment of care. PMH includes HTN, DM type 2, HLD, senile dementia, CKD with recent hyponatremia Patient is unable to providea complete medical history due to her advance dementia, husband and son are with patient to provide additonal information. They report patient had been with home hospice and discharged last week due to improving condition. She had experienced a recent fall and was found to have significant hyponatremia and corrected with Sodium tablets 1000mg twice daily. She has an appointment to see her nephrologist at Kidney Specialists of Southern Nevada this week. Son states her mood has been stable, no outbursts, no aggression, no signs of depression. She mostly sits in her recliner all day and watches television. She ambulates with a cane or walker for assistance. Sleep has been good on Trazodone. Appetite has been fair. No coughing or choking noted when eating. Their main challenge has been with her personal hygiene, particularly bathing, requiring family assistance and extensive encouragement to shower approximately every two weeks. She performs basic washing at sink with assistance. Patient is agreeable to visits from home care. SH: Lives with husband and son in a 2 storey house, patient stays on the first floor. Non- smoker, non-alcoholic beverage drinker. Medications LOSARTAN/HCTZ 100-12.5MG TABLET, TAKE 1 TABLET BY MOUTH EVERY MORNING TRAZODONE 50 MG TABLET SIMVASTATIN 20 MG TABLET NIFEDIPINE ER 30 MG TABLET MEMANTINE HCL 5 MG TABLET JANUVIA 50 MG TABLET AMLODIPINE BESYLATE 10 MG TAB SODIUM CHLORIDE 1000 MG TABLET, TAKE 1 TABLET BY MOUTH IN THE MORNING AND 1 TABLET BY MOUTH IN THE EVENING. Allergies No known allergies Mental/Functional The patient's speech was normal, sharing conversation with normal laryngeal efforts. Appropriate mood and affect were seen on exam.
